H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H-OS-L1 (Pebble) serves the largest HEALING cohort, 96 listeners, with a mean outcome of 7.10 (std 1.82) and pronounced right‑skew toward higher scores. Strong confidence is evident (pct_pos = 66 %). Qualitative feedback notes commute‑related usage and 5‑10 minute settle intervals. YouTube reach is 29 views; retention metrics remain unavailable. AutoGluon predictions match observed results.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Pebble is a strong tool for overstimulation, with most feeling noticeable improvement and liking the sound.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: Overstimulation track H-OS-L2 engages 52 listeners, producing a mean of 6.83 (std 1.84) with a narrow spread. Confidence is strong (pct_pos = 63 %). Qualitative comments include a split music reception and typical settle times of 5‑10 minutes. YouTube views are five, limiting external insight. Model predictions agree with observed performance.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Rainfall smooths overstimulation reliably, though music opinions are split.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: Glacier protocol serves 47 users, achieving a mean of 7.04 (std 1.68) and a bimodal‑resembling distribution. Confidence is meaningful (pct_pos = 64 %). Qualitative suggests high adoption and quick settles, though some report rhythm complexity issues. YouTube views total eight. Predictions converge with observed scores.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Glacier caps overstimulation support, delivering steady calm and quick settling.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: Protocol H-AX-L1 enrolls 67 listeners, yielding a mean outcome score of 7.22 (std 1.93) with a right‑skewed distribution concentrated at scores 8–9. Confidence is strong given n ≥ 50 and pct_pos = 63 %. Qualitative feedback from 23 respondents highlights self‑directed use and therapy contexts, with noticeably smoother settles around 5‑10 minutes. YouTube reaches only five views, limiting external visibility. AutoGluon predictions align with observed mean, indicating consistent efficacy.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: The Meadow track reliably helps anxiety and stress, with most listeners feeling a noticeable lift within 5‑10 minutes. Many use it personally or with a patient and love the calming rhythm.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: H-AX-L2 serves 48 participants with a mean of 6.94 (std 1.62) and modest variance, reflecting central clustering around scores 7–8. Confidence is meaningful (pct_pos = 67 %). Qualitative reports include mixed music reception and neutral opinions, yet rhythmic comfort is noted. YouTube views remain at six, offering minimal engagement data. Model predictions confirm observed performance.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Cottage delivers similar calm, though some find the music merely okay. Still, it settles quickly for most and gets good scores.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: With 36 listeners, H-AX-L3 achieves a meaningful mean of 7.08 (std 1.89), showing slight left skew but no extreme lows. Confidence tier is meaningful (pct_pos = 61 %). Qualitative input flags a subset of "no effect" responses despite overall positive trends. YouTube exposure is limited to four views. AutoGluon predictions agree with observed scores.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Valley continues the anxiety work well, but a few didn't notice any change.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Crisis protocol H-CR-L1 engages 43 users in acute distress, delivering a mean of 6.35 (std 1.70) with moderate spread, consistent with structural expectations. Confidence is meaningful (pct_pos = 53 %). Qualitative data shows immediate settle reports but also a minority noting adverse effects. YouTube views total six. Model forecasts match observed outcomes.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Anchor is built for crisis moments and brings modest relief, though a small group felt worse afterward.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: Protocol H-CR-L2 records 35 crisis participants, achieving a mean of 6.40 (std 1.72) and bimodal distribution hints at subgroup effects. Confidence is meaningful (pct_pos = 51 %). Qualitative mentions include neutral music opinions and mixed settle times. YouTube view count is four. Predictions converge with observed data.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Harbor helps in crises but some find the music too busy; a few never settle.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: H-CR-L3 supports 27 users in crisis, with a mean of 6.52 (std 1.91) and 52 % positive responses. Distribution clusters near mid‑range values. Qualitative inputs report improving trends for many but also isolated "no effect" cases. YouTube views are five. AutoGluon predictions align with observed performance.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Horizon shows a bit better relief for crisis users, with many feeling help fast.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: Sleep track H-SL-L1 supports 55 listeners, delivering a mean of 7.16 (std 1.85) and a moderately dispersed distribution. Confidence is strong. Qualitative reports indicate therapeutic usage and 5‑minute settle patterns. YouTube views are four. AutoGluon predictions align with observed outcomes.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Lantern gently guides toward sleep, often effective within five minutes.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: Hammock protocol engages 45 listeners, achieving a mean of 7.09 (std 1.55) and tight clustering around mid‑range scores. Confidence is meaningful. Qualitative includes caregiver‑derived reports of varied settle experiences and mixed music opinions. YouTube views remain at five. Predictions converge.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Hammock offers reliable sleep aid, though a few never quite settle.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: Midnight protocol serves 29 listeners, with a mean of 7.10 (std 1.65) and a concentrated distribution. Confidence is meaningful. Qualitative notes rhythmic complexities and some non‑settling reports. YouTube views are three. Model predictions align with observed data.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Midnight provides gentle sleep support with familiar rhythms.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: Compass (Focus & Clarity L1) engages 41 listeners, achieving a mean of 6.46 (std 1.82) with atypical distribution showing a secondary peak at score 5. Confidence is meaningful. Qualitative reports mixed music reception and moderate improvement signals. YouTube views are five. AutoGluon predictions agree with observed outcome.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Compass for focus falls short of expectations; some find the rhythm off.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: Waypoint (Focus & Clarity L2) serves 32 participants with a mean of 7.19 (std 1.60) and favorable distribution clustering. Confidence is meaningful. Qualitative shows strong institutional adoption and noticeable improvement signals. YouTube views are four. Predictions converge.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Waypoint lifts focus scores noticeably, especially among professionals.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: Summit (Focus & Clarity L3) engages 34 listeners, achieving the highest THRIVING mean of 7.56 (std 1.96) with a bimodal‑like spread and 71 % positive responses. Confidence is meaningful. Qualitative confirms rapid effect for many and high music enthusiasm. YouTube views are fifteen, the strongest reach among THRIVING protocols. AutoGluon predictions align, though ceiling‑effect concerns arise.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Summit delivers the strongest clarity boost reported, praised for instant effect.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: Creative Flow L1 (Ember) enrolls 35 participants, producing a mean of 6.74 (std 1.70) with moderate variability. Confidence is meaningful. Qualitative indicates modest to negligible improvement for many. YouTube views are two, offering minimal external exposure. AutoGluon predictions align with observed performance.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Ember nudges creative flow modestly; not everyone feels a big boost.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: Focus & Clarity L2 (Current) serves 25 listeners, achieving a mean of 6.72 (std 1.43) and low variance. Confidence is meaningful. Qualitative highlights strong music affinity and moderate improvement reports. YouTube views are two. Predictions converge with observed data.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Current fine‑tunes the approach and many love its music.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: Aurora (Creative Flow L3) engages 18 listeners, yielding a mean of 7.28 (std 1.32) and high confidence emerging from a tight score distribution. Qualitative shows noticeable improvements and rapid settles for completers. YouTube views are seven, indicating modest reach. Model predictions align with observed mean.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Aurora sparks creativity for the small group that completes it, showing strong results.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: Maintenance & Balance L1 (Stone) supports 26 listeners, with a mean of 7.08 (std 1.32) and tight clustering near scores 7–8. Confidence is meaningful. Qualitative indicates moderate improvement but persistent settle times exceeding ten minutes for some. YouTube views are three. AutoGluon predictions agree with observed score.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Stone aims to keep daily balance steady; results stay moderate across users.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: Maintenance & Balance L2 (Axis) engages 22 listeners, achieving a mean of 6.68 (std 2.03) and broader variance. Confidence is meaningful. Qualitative shows polarized reception and mixed music opinions. YouTube views are four. Predictions align with observed performance.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Axis shows mixed outcomes, working better for some than others.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: Orbit (Maintenance & Balance L3) serves 14 listeners, with a mean of 6.86 (std 1.70) and high positive sentiment in qualitative feedback. Confidence is developing due to small sample. YouTube views are three. Model predictions align with observed data.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Orbit remains a small, early test but leans positive.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: Breeze (Mood & Energy L1) enrolls 38 listeners, with a mean of 6.68 (std 1.93) and moderate distribution. Confidence is meaningful. Qualitative shows caregiver‑laden usage and strong music affinity. YouTube views are five. Predictions align with observed outcome.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Breeze reliably lifts mood and energy, often used by caregivers.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: Sunrise (Mood & Energy L2) serves 24 listeners, achieving a mean of 6.71 (std 1.73) and consistent positivity. Confidence is meaningful. Qualitative confirms similar improvements and moderate settle times. YouTube views are eight. Predictions converge.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Sunrise offers similar energy gains with moderate reports.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: Updraft (Mood & Energy L3) supports 17 listeners, with a mean of 6.24 (std 1.79) and modest distribution. Confidence is developing. Qualitative notes varied efficacy and task‑related usage. YouTube views are four. Predictions align with observed data.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Updraft provides modest energy lift, good for light tasks.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: Footpath (Motivation & Drive L1) engages 21 listeners, with a mean of 6.24 (std 1.97) and low positivity. Confidence is meaningful. Qualitative indicates noticeable improvements but suggests protocol may be under‑dosed. YouTube views are four. Predictions align with observed outcome.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Footpath tries to raise motivation but stays flat for many.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: Trail (Motivation & Drive L2) supports 14 listeners, achieving a mean of 6.00 (std 1.57) and the lowest positivity overall. Confidence is developing. Qualitative shows limited efficacy and prolonged settle times. YouTube views are seven. Predictions converge with observed performance.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Trail barely moves motivation scores; redesign needed.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: Switchback (Motivation & Drive L3) serves 12 listeners, with a mean of 6.92 (std 2.31) and high variance. Confidence is developing. Qualitative indicates some improvement but also variability. YouTube views are seven. Predictions align with observed data.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Switchback shows a jump up for a few, though data is thin.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: Cavern protocol enrolls 23 listeners, delivering a mean of 6.65 (std 1.58) with a conventional distribution. Confidence is meaningful. Qualitative includes occasional adverse reports despite overall positive music reception. YouTube views are eight. AutoGluon predictions align with observed outcomes.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Cavern offers mind clarity but some feel discomfort; rhythm may be too intense.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: Starlight (Mind & Clarity L2) serves 15 listeners, achieving a mean of 6.60 (std 1.45) and moderate positivity. Confidence is developing. Qualitative shows strong music liking and varied settle experiences. YouTube views are eleven. Predictions converge with observed scores.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Starlight blends with mood tracks, showing consistent reception.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: Cosmos (Mind & Clarity L3) supports only nine listeners, yielding a low mean of 5.33 (std 2.29) and high variance. Confidence is developing. Qualitative reports include rhythm‑busy concerns and a minority noting worsening effects. YouTube views are eight. Predictions diverge from observed mean, suggesting potential redesign.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Cosmos is too intense for some, dragging the overall score down.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The data set captures 1000 outcome scores across 30 protocols in the HEALING, THRIVING, and TRANSFORMING series.
• HEALING and THRIVING show comparable means around 6.95, while TRANSFORMING trails at 6.47 expectedly.
• TRANSFORMING exhibits the highest variability, reflecting its ascending-arc challenge.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series-level patterns demonstrate distinct progression intents.
• HEALING DESCENT protocols maintain steady outcomes above 6.8.
• THRIVING HOLD protocols vary, with T-FC-L3 peaking at 7.56.
• TRANSFORMING ASCENT protocols show mixed performance, including low positivity in X-MD-L2.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols deviate from expected patterns.
• X-MC-L3 mean 5.33 with n=9 and pct_pos=44% signals low efficacy.
• T-FC-L1 mean 6.46 lags same-series peers despite standard design.
• X-MD-L2 mean 6.0 and pct_pos=36% indicate subgroup challenge.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Patterns suggest targeted refinements.
• T-FC-L3 (n=34) may benefit from an L2 variant before its rise.
• X-MD-L2 (n=14) low positivity may require ascent gradient adjustment.
• H-CR-L1 (n=43) modest mean could improve with routing tweaks.
• X-MC-L3 (n=9) outliers warrant redesign of low-intensity cohort support.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding signals indicate analytical evolution.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: 29 of 30 protocols show similarity below 0.85.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL CLUSTERS: HEALING pain points group tightly; X-MD-L1 remains most isolated.
• Confidence in drift metrics is moderate given recent data influx.
